name: SCI Engineered Materials, Inc.
business: SCI Engineered Materials, Inc. is a global supplier and manufacturer of advanced materials for Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD) thin film applications. It serves markets including Aerospace, Automotive, Defense, Glass, Optical Coatings, and Solar. Substantially all revenues are generated from customers with multinational operations.

exposures.sentence: SCI utilizes its manufacturing footprint and industry expertise for defense applications including Enriched Boron Carbide.
Enriched Boron Carbide has several attractive properties which involve high-temperature applications in the aerospace and nuclear markets.
Enriched Boron Carbide has several attractive properties which involve high-temperature applications in the aerospace and nuclear markets.
We are focused on markets within the PVD industry including Aerospace, Automotive, Defense, Glass, Optical Coatings, and Solar.
The Company’s 2025 strategic review led to an increased commitment to spherical powders, which are utilized in additive manufacturing, powder metallurgy, and other advanced manufacturing processes.

We continue to invest in developing new applications for our markets including electrically conductive Indium Tin Oxide and Zinc Tin Oxide for the architectural glass, automotive, defense, display, and solar markets.
Our competitors may be larger, more diversified, better funded, and have access to more advanced technology, including Artificial Intelligence (AI).
Our business could be negatively impacted by cyber and other security threats or disruptions.

Changes in global economic conditions could adversely affect our business, which include, but are not limited to, international tariffs, inflation, interest rates, supply chain disruptions, and continued global semiconductor chip shortages.

catalysts.event: Introduced two new products during the second quarter of 2025
catalysts.sentence: This decrease was due to fewer research materials and supplies of $140,187 as the Company introduced two new products during the second quarter of 2025.

catalysts.event: Increased manufacturing for spherical powders in 2026
catalysts.sentence: This complements SCI’s established capabilities and will be supported by increased manufacturing in 2026.

catalysts.event: Initiated share repurchase program during the fourth quarter of 2025
catalysts.sentence: We initiated a share repurchase program during the fourth quarter of 2025 and $500,000 of SCI’s common stock was purchased as of December 31, 2025.

catalysts.event: Line of credit expires on August 29, 2026
catalysts.sentence: The Company has a line of credit with a regional bank for $1 million which expires on August 29, 2026.

catalysts.event: CFO Jerry Blaskie plans to retire on April 1, 2026
catalysts.sentence: As announced in September 2025, Jerry Blaskie, the Company’s Vice President, Chief Financial Officer, Treasurer and Assistant Secretary, plans to retire on April 1, 2026.
